pending.body: Fix the SIGTERM handoff so it does not append a durable `run_failed` journal event before the engine future finishes. In `src/cli/run.rs:2098-2103` and `src/cli/run.rs:2146-2175`, the wrapper persists interruption state via `mark_running_run_interrupted()` and then still allows the engine future to continue. If the engine later reaches its normal failure path, it appends another terminal event using its stale in-memory `seq` counter at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5987-6000`, which can write a duplicate or out-of-order journal sequence and corrupt `journal.ndjson`.

Do not treat every dead tracked backend leader as a hard cleanup failure. `kill_stale_backend_process_group()` now errors immediately when `is_backend_process_alive(record)` is false (`src/cli/run.rs:2622-2626`), and stale recovery invokes that path before it can repair the snapshot (`src/cli/run.rs:2793-2795`). If a backend already exited cleanly but its `active-processes.json` entry survived a crash or forced stop, `run resume` on a stale Running snapshot fails even though there is nothing left to kill.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
